faults.name: Incorrect base bias drives the transistor into saturation
faults.why: Too much base bias current can pull the collector close to the emitter and remove linear gain.
faults.verification_test: Measure collector, base, and emitter DC voltages before applying AC input.
faults.fix_recipe: Resize the bias divider or emitter resistor so the collector sits near mid-supply.

faults.name: Emitter resistor or bypass path is open
faults.why: An open emitter path changes DC bias and can stop the amplifier from operating in the active region.
faults.verification_test: Measure emitter voltage and check continuity through RE.
faults.fix_recipe: Reconnect the emitter resistor and verify the DC operating point.

faults.name: Collector resistor path is open
faults.why: Without a collector load path, the output node cannot develop the expected voltage swing.
faults.verification_test: Power off and check continuity from VCC through RC to the collector.
faults.fix_recipe: Reconnect RC to VCC and the collector node.
